title: Parameterized Access, Authorization, Privacy, and Audit · Query Retrieval
summary: A web service accepts user filters, exposes support data by role, and must resist injection and excessive collection. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a least-privilege parameterized data-access contract after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify code-data boundary failures in dynamic SQL and demonstrate why escaping alone is incomplete.
Bind untrusted values through prepared statements while allow-listing unavoidable identifiers or directions.
Design separate identities, roles, grants, views, and row or column controls for minimum required authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Audit Provenance
steps.instruction:
review case — A viewer submits severity text "high' OR 1=1 --" and sort direction "desc" while requesting member details. Bind values, allow-list structure, minimize returned fields, apply least privilege, and redact audit evidence. Competency probe: Record actor, authority, purpose, input provenance, query or change identity, and tamper-evident outcomes without logging secrets.

Inspect the Audit Provenance trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Audit Provenance, keep Audit Provenance connected to executable relational evidence.
